pattern: Each vowel at position i contributes to exactly (i+1)*(n-i) substrings (one choice of start at or before it, one choice of end at or after it), so summing that weight over every vowel position gives the total in one linear pass.
hints: Instead of building every substring, ask for each character: how many substrings contain it at all?
A character at index i is contained in a substring for every combination of a start index in [0, i] and an end index in [i, n-1], so it contributes (i+1) * (n-i) times whenever it's a vowel.
Sum that weight over every vowel position for the final total -- watch out for integer overflow in languages that need it, though a plain double/number is safe here.

edge_inputs.description: a short mixed string with one vowel repeated at both ends
one vowel followed by two consonants
every character a distinct vowel, maximizing density
a long run of the same vowel repeated, exercising the weighting formula at scale

stress.description: random lowercase letters at the max string length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
